content.problemStatement:
An array BBB of length MMM is said to be good, if and only if for every iii such that 1ÔëĄi<M1 \le i \lt M1ÔëĄi<M, ÔłúBiÔłĺBi+1ÔłúÔëĄM|B_i - B_{i + 1}| \le MÔłúBiÔÇőÔłĺBi+1ÔÇőÔłúÔëĄM holds.

That is, the absolute difference between any 222 adjacent elements does not exceed the length of the array.

Given an array AAA of length NNN, count the number of its subarrays that are good.

Formally, count the number of pairs (L,R)(L, R)(L,R) (1ÔëĄLÔëĄRÔëĄN1 \le L \le R \le N1ÔëĄLÔëĄRÔëĄN) such that the array [AL,AL+1,...,AR][A_L, A_{L + 1}, ..., A_R][ALÔÇő,AL+1ÔÇő,...,ARÔÇő] is good.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN, the size of the array.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, A1,A2,ÔÇŽ,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1ÔÇő,A2ÔÇő,ÔÇŽ,ANÔÇő, the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subarrays.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2Ôőů1052 \cdot 10^52Ôőů105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: It can be seen that all subarrays are good.
Test case 222: The subarrays [1,4][1, 4][1,4] and [4,1][4, 1][4,1] are not good, since Ôłú4Ôłĺ1Ôłú=3>2|4-1| = 3 \gt 2Ôłú4Ôłĺ1Ôłú=3>2.
The other four subarrays are good.
Test case 333: [1,4][1, 4][1,4] is the only subarray that isn't good.
